fields.Text:
32-year-old woman with dilated ovarian/pelvic veins treated with ovarian vein coils. Most likely condition?

{{c1::Pelvic venous insufficiency}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Estrogen induces venous dilation via nitric oxide release while progesterone weakens venous valves, collectively promoting ovarian vein incompetence and reflux in pelvic venous insufficiency, particularly in multiparous, premenopausal women.

fields.Text: Pelvic venous insufficiency is promoted by hormonal venous dilation and valve weakness, especially in {{c1::multiparous premenopausal women}}.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Estrogen induces venous dilation via nitric oxide release while progesterone weakens venous valves, collectively promoting ovarian vein incompetence and reflux in pelvic venous insufficiency, particularly in multiparous, premenopausal women.

fields.Text: PET spatial resolution is best in {{c1::bone}} because high tissue density limits positron range before annihilation.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Bone provides the best spatial resolution in PET imaging due to its high density which limits positron range, while lung tissue provides the worst resolution due to its low density allowing greater positron travel before annihilation.

fields.Text:
Hypercoagulable young woman with abdominal pain and thrombus in the porta hepatis. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Portal vein thrombosis}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. A large thrombus is seen in the portahepatis within a distended portal vein.

fields.Text: A thrombus within a distended portal vein is diagnostic of {{c1::portal vein thrombosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: easy. A large thrombus is seen in the portahepatis within a distended portal vein.

fields.Text: The breast MRI enhancement curve most suggestive of malignancy is {{c1::rapid initial uptake with washout}}.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: easy. There are three possible patterns of enhancement that have been described for breast lesions after administration of gadolinium.

fields.Text:
Male mammogram with bilateral retroareolar flame-shaped masses. BI-RADS assessment?

{{c1::BI-RADS 2}}
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Bilateral gynecomastia on mammography typically receives BI-RADS 2 classification, but BI-RADS 3 lesions in men have higher malignancy risk than in women and require close monitoring.

fields.Text: Typical bilateral gynecomastia on mammography is a benign finding classified as {{c1::BI-RADS 2}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Bilateral gynecomastia on mammography typically receives BI-RADS 2 classification, but BI-RADS 3 lesions in men have higher malignancy risk than in women and require close monitoring.

fields.Text:
Breast cancer patient with dyspnea and fissural fluid on V/Q-correlative imaging. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Pleural effusion}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. There is a large pleural-based opacity with fluid tracking along the upper lateral margin of the pleura.

fields.Text: A fissure sign on V/Q imaging can be caused by {{c1::pleural effusion}} tracking in the fissure.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. There is a large pleural-based opacity with fluid tracking along the upper lateral margin of the pleura.

fields.Text: In a cancer patient with dyspnea, a fissural defect on V/Q without mismatch can represent {{c1::pleural effusion}}, not pulmonary embolism.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. There is a large pleural-based opacity with fluid tracking along the upper lateral margin of the pleura.

fields.Text: Mammography CAD should be used as a {{c1::second look/adjuvant}} after the radiologist's initial interpretation.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: CAD in mammography was designed as "second opinion" but shows mixed clinical results - effective use requires balancing workflow efficiency with diagnostic accuracy, as improper implementation can increase missed cancers or false-positives.

fields.Text:
Skull base foramina image: which labeled structure transmits the middle meningeal artery?

{{c1::Foramen spinosum}}
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: easy. Explanation: This is foramen spinosum.

fields.Text: The middle meningeal artery passes through the {{c1::foramen spinosum}}.
fields.Extra: Q8. Correct; difficulty: easy. Explanation: This is foramen spinosum.

fields.Text:
2-year-old with short femurs and absent proximal femoral ossification centers. Most likely condition?

{{c1::Proximal focal femoral deficiency}}
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Proximal focal femoral deficiency on radiograph shows a shortened femur with absence or hypoplasia of the proximal femoral segment, often lacking a normal femoral head and neck, and associated acetabular dysplasia.

fields.Text: Proximal focal femoral deficiency may show absent proximal femoral ossification centers until {{c1::2 to 3 years of age}}.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Proximal focal femoral deficiency on radiograph shows a shortened femur with absence or hypoplasia of the proximal femoral segment, often lacking a normal femoral head and neck, and associated acetabular dysplasia.

fields.Text: A systems approach to medical errors emphasizes {{c1::reporting/analyzing errors and preventing similar events}} rather than blaming individuals alone.
fields.Extra: Q10. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: As stated in the 2025 ABR NIS Guide, physicians should be committed to reporting and analyzing medical mistakes to develop appropriate prevention and improvement strategies.

fields.Text:
Infant skeletal survey with narrowing interpedicular distances and squared iliac wings. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Achondroplasia}}
fields.Extra: Q11. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The frontal radiograph shows progressive narrowing of the interpedicular distances (blue arrows) as well as squared iliac wings (yellow arrows) and horizontal acetabular roofs (red arrows).

fields.Text: Achondroplasia classically shows {{c1::progressive narrowing of lumbar interpedicular distances}} with squared iliac wings and horizontal acetabular roofs.
fields.Extra: Q11. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The frontal radiograph shows progressive narrowing of the interpedicular distances (blue arrows) as well as squared iliac wings (yellow arrows) and horizontal acetabular roofs (red arrows).

fields.Text: Achondroplasia is the most common skeletal dysplasia and classically has {{c1::progressive narrowing of interpedicular distances}}.
fields.Extra: Q11. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The frontal radiograph shows progressive narrowing of the interpedicular distances (blue arrows) as well as squared iliac wings (yellow arrows) and horizontal acetabular roofs (red arrows).

fields.Text: A pinhole collimator {{c1::magnifies and inverts}} gamma camera images.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Pinhole collimators produce inverted images with magnification determined by distance ratios - magnified when image-to-pinhole distance exceeds object-to-pinhole distance.

fields.Text: A {{c1::tangential mammographic view}} confirms dermal localization of suspected skin calcifications.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Dermal calcifications result from processes such as folliculitis or inspissated material within sweat glands. To specifically characterize dermal calcifications, a tangential mammographic view is advised.

fields.Text:
Liver mass with peripheral nodular discontinuous enhancement and progressive fill-in. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hemangioma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Large hepatic hemangiomas typically show lobulated margins and become inhomogeneous due to degenerative changes and thrombosis but maintain the characteristic peripheral discontinuous globular enhancement with progressive centripetal filling that distinguishes them from malignant mimickers.

fields.Text: Hepatic hemangioma shows peripheral discontinuous nodular enhancement with {{c1::progressive centripetal fill-in}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Large hepatic hemangiomas typically show lobulated margins and become inhomogeneous due to degenerative changes and thrombosis but maintain the characteristic peripheral discontinuous globular enhancement with progressive centripetal filling that distinguishes them from malignant mimickers.

fields.Text: Paget disease of the breast is caused by malignant cells infiltrating the {{c1::nipple and adjacent skin}} and is usually associated with underlying breast cancer.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: easy. Paget disease of the breast causes skin findings that may seem benign at first glance.

fields.Text:
Post-EVAR aneurysm sac enhancement from collateral inflow. Endoleak type?

{{c1::Type 2 endoleak}}
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The types of endoleak are classified by the source of the leak. The type of endoleak determines the magnitude of intrasac pressure.

fields.Text: A type 2 endoleak is caused by {{c1::retrograde collateral branch-vessel flow}} into the aneurysm sac.
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The types of endoleak are classified by the source of the leak. The type of endoleak determines the magnitude of intrasac pressure.

fields.Text:
Post-C-section patient with heterogeneous collection between bladder and lower uterine segment. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Bladder flap hematoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The initial sagittal ultrasound images at the level of the bladder show a large, heterogeneous mass with lace-like internal echoes (green arrow) between the lower uterine segment (blue arrow) and the bladder wall (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: A bladder flap hematoma after C-section lies between the {{c1::lower uterine segment and bladder}}.
fields.Extra: Q17. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The initial sagittal ultrasound images at the level of the bladder show a large, heterogeneous mass with lace-like internal echoes (green arrow) between the lower uterine segment (blue arrow) and the bladder wall (yellow arrow).

fields.Text:
Ultrasound with repeated equidistant near-field lines from parallel reflectors. What artifact is shown?

{{c1::Reverberation artifact}}
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The repeated near-field lines represent reverberation artifact from beam bouncing between transducer face and anterior wall of the fluid structure, with each reflection cycle creating apparent echoes at progressively deeper locations due to cumulative transit time delays.

fields.Text: Reverberation artifact creates {{c1::multiple equally spaced linear echoes}} from repeated reflections between parallel surfaces.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The repeated near-field lines represent reverberation artifact from beam bouncing between transducer face and anterior wall of the fluid structure, with each reflection cycle creating apparent echoes at progressively deeper locations due to cumulative transit time delays.

fields.Text:
Multiple dural-based enhancing masses plus spinal intradural lesion in a familial tumor syndrome. Expected associated tumor?

{{c1::Schwannoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Neurofibromatosis type 2 (NF2) is a rare autosomal dominant neurocutaneous disorder (phakomatosis) characterizes by development of multiple CNS tumors such as intracranial schwannomas (mostly vestibular schwannomas and rarely spinal schwannomas), intracranial and spinal meningiomas, and intraspinal-intramedullary ependymomas.

fields.Text: Neurofibromatosis type 2 is associated with multiple CNS tumors, especially {{c1::vestibular or spinal schwannomas}} and meningiomas.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Neurofibromatosis type 2 (NF2) is a rare autosomal dominant neurocutaneous disorder (phakomatosis) characterizes by development of multiple CNS tumors such as intracranial schwannomas (mostly vestibular schwannomas and rarely spinal schwannomas), intracranial and spinal meningiomas, and intraspinal-intramedullary ependymomas.

fields.Text:
Twin pregnancy ultrasound with two separate gestational sacs. Chorionicity/amnionicity?

{{c1::Dichorionic diamniotic}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. There are two clearly separate gestational sacs in the endometrium, compatible with a dichorionic-diamniotic pregnancy.

fields.Text: Two separate gestational sacs indicate a {{c1::dichorionic diamniotic}} twin pregnancy.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. There are two clearly separate gestational sacs in the endometrium, compatible with a dichorionic-diamniotic pregnancy.

fields.Text:
Infant bone scan with multiple posterior rib fractures. What should this raise concern for?

{{c1::Non-accidental trauma}}
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Rib fractures are injuries strongly associated with child abuse. In the absence of a history of massive accidental trauma, underlying bone fragility or cardiopulmonary resuscitation, rib fractures are likely to be due to nonaccidental injury.

fields.Text: Multiple posterior rib fractures in an infant are highly specific for {{c1::non-accidental trauma}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Rib fractures are injuries strongly associated with child abuse. In the absence of a history of massive accidental trauma, underlying bone fragility or cardiopulmonary resuscitation, rib fractures are likely to be due to nonaccidental injury.

fields.Text: Increasing NEX improves MRI SNR by the {{c1::square root of the NEX}} while increasing scan time proportionally.
fields.Extra: Q22. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: SNR in spin-echo is affected by the intrinsic signal of the tissue, the voxel size, NEX and bandwidth.

fields.Text: Increasing NEX improves SNR by {{c1::sqrt(NEX)}}; increasing from 1 to 4 NEX doubles SNR but quadruples scan time.
fields.Extra: Q22. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: SNR in spin-echo is affected by the intrinsic signal of the tissue, the voxel size, NEX and bandwidth.

fields.Text:
Young woman with arterially enhancing liver lesion and central scar. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Focal nodular hyperplasia}}
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. Focal nodular hyperplasia (FNH) is essentially a liver hamartoma (disorganized but nonmalignant hepatocytes), typified by a T2 signal that is isointense to slightly hyperintense to background liver parenchyma.

fields.Text: Focal nodular hyperplasia is a benign hepatic hamartoma with arterial enhancement and often a {{c1::central scar}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: easy. Focal nodular hyperplasia (FNH) is essentially a liver hamartoma (disorganized but nonmalignant hepatocytes), typified by a T2 signal that is isointense to slightly hyperintense to background liver parenchyma.

fields.Text:
Older man with intravesical prostatic protrusion and increased Doppler vascularity. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::BPH with prostatitis}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with intravesical protrusion indicates significant obstruction that may require intervention. Coexisting prostatitis presents with increased vascularity on color Doppler.

fields.Text: BPH with intravesical protrusion suggests significant obstruction, and prostatitis may show {{c1::increased color Doppler flow}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with intravesical protrusion indicates significant obstruction that may require intervention. Coexisting prostatitis presents with increased vascularity on color Doppler.

fields.Text:
Lower-extremity venous US with failure of venous collapse on compression. Most important DVT finding?

{{c1::Lack of wall coaptation with compression}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) most commonly occurs in the lower limbs. Radiologic features are: non-compressible venous segment, loss of phasic flow on Valsalva maneuver, absent color flow if completely occlusive, lack of flow augmentation with calf squeeze, increased flow in superficial veins.

fields.Text: The primary ultrasound sign of lower-extremity DVT is {{c1::noncompressibility/lack of venous wall coaptation}}.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) most commonly occurs in the lower limbs. Radiologic features are: non-compressible venous segment, loss of phasic flow on Valsalva maneuver, absent color flow if completely occlusive, lack of flow augmentation with calf squeeze, increased flow in superficial veins.

fields.Text:
Penetrating anterior chest trauma with hemopericardium. Most likely injury?

{{c1::Cardiac laceration}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: hard. Close inspection of the given image reveals a skin defect with a small amount of subcutaneous gas in a linear arrangement in the anterior chest wall anterior to the heart (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: Penetrating anterior chest trauma with hemopericardium is highly suspicious for {{c1::cardiac laceration}}.
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: hard. Close inspection of the given image reveals a skin defect with a small amount of subcutaneous gas in a linear arrangement in the anterior chest wall anterior to the heart (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: A major radioactive spill includes more than {{c1::1 mCi of I-131}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Cut-off values differentiating major and minor spills for different radionuclides is highly testable for CORE. A more comprehensive list of cut-off values is present in the ABR RISC Study Guide.

fields.Text:
Portal vein Doppler initially measured at 90 degrees then normal after angle correction. Explanation?

{{c1::Doppler angle artifact}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: At a 90° Doppler angle, cosine equals zero, making velocity calculation impossible since the ultrasound beam is perpendicular to flow direction no frequency shift occurs because there's no velocity component toward or away from the transducer.

fields.Text: At a 90-degree Doppler angle, cosine is zero, so velocity calculation fails and can falsely show {{c1::no flow}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: At a 90° Doppler angle, cosine equals zero, making velocity calculation impossible since the ultrasound beam is perpendicular to flow direction no frequency shift occurs because there's no velocity component toward or away from the transducer.

fields.Text:
Knee MRI with reciprocal edema in lateral femoral condyle and medial patellar facet. Recent injury?

{{c1::Lateral patellar translocation}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: easy. The axial fat-saturated T2 image shows a reciprocal marrow edema lateral femoral condyle (red arrow) and the medial patellar facet (orange arrow).

fields.Text: Lateral patellar dislocation causes reciprocal marrow edema at the {{c1::lateral femoral condyle and medial patellar facet}}.
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: easy. The axial fat-saturated T2 image shows a reciprocal marrow edema lateral femoral condyle (red arrow) and the medial patellar facet (orange arrow).

fields.Text: Daily PET uniformity quality control typically uses {{c1::germanium-68 or sodium-22}}.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A uniformity scan on PET is performed every day and typically utilizes germanium-68 or sodium-22. Also, cobalt-57 would be an optimal flood source for a uniformity scan.

fields.Text: Daily PET uniformity QC uses {{c1::germanium-68}} or sodium-22, not a standard gamma-camera cobalt flood source.
fields.Extra: Q30. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: A uniformity scan on PET is performed every day and typically utilizes germanium-68 or sodium-22. Also, cobalt-57 would be an optimal flood source for a uniformity scan.

fields.Text: Gamma camera intrinsic uniformity testing is performed {{c1::daily}} before patient imaging.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Daily intrinsic uniformity testing is mandatory before patient imaging to detect and eliminate non-uniformities that cause artifacts and false-positive or false-negative results. This quality control measure ensures consistent detector response across the field of view and identifies problems immediately before they compromise diagnostic accuracy.

fields.Text:
Internal auditory canal MRI: which labeled nerve carries balance information?

{{c1::Vestibular nerve}}
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The sagittal T2 weighted MRI image at the level of the internal auditory canal profiles the vestibular nerve in short-axis, as indicated by the blue arrow.

fields.Text: Balance information from the utricle, saccule, and semicircular canals is carried by the {{c1::vestibular nerve}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Correct; difficulty: not listed. The sagittal T2 weighted MRI image at the level of the internal auditory canal profiles the vestibular nerve in short-axis, as indicated by the blue arrow.

fields.Text:
Angiography in a young patient with aortic root disease and marfanoid disorder. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Marfan syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Marfan syndrome is a genetic abnormality of collagen formation resulting from a defect in the fibrillin gene.

fields.Text: Marfan syndrome results from a defect in {{c1::fibrillin}} and is associated with aortic root disease.
fields.Extra: Q33. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Marfan syndrome is a genetic abnormality of collagen formation resulting from a defect in the fibrillin gene.

fields.Text: STIR provides reliable fat suppression near metal because it depends on {{c1::T1 relaxation time rather than resonance frequency}}.
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Metal implants alter proton resonance frequencies but do not change intrinsic T1 relaxation times. Frequency-based fat suppression techniques fail near metal because they target specific frequencies that become shifted, while STIR remains effective because it relies on unchanged T1 relaxation differences between fat and water rather than frequency differences.

fields.Text:
Well-circumscribed pancreatic mass with solid/cystic components and thick enhancing capsule. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm}}
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: SPN is a rare low-grade malignant neoplasm predominantly affecting young women with excellent prognosis.

fields.Text: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm is a rare low-grade malignant pancreatic tumor classically affecting {{c1::young women}}.
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: SPN is a rare low-grade malignant neoplasm predominantly affecting young women with excellent prognosis.

fields.Text:
Shoulder radiograph with supraspinatus calcific tendinosis. Mineral type?

{{c1::Calcium hydroxyapatite}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This frontal externally rotated shoulder radiograph shows two foci of calcification superior to the greater tuberosity (blue arrows), consistent with supraspinatus calcific tendonosis.

fields.Text: Calcific tendinosis of the rotator cuff is due to {{c1::calcium hydroxyapatite}} deposition.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. This frontal externally rotated shoulder radiograph shows two foci of calcification superior to the greater tuberosity (blue arrows), consistent with supraspinatus calcific tendonosis.

fields.Text:
Child with facial port-wine stain, seizures, and cortical calcification/atrophy. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Sturge-Weber syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. These are classic findings in Sturge-Weber syndrome.

fields.Text: Sturge-Weber syndrome classically combines a facial port-wine stain with {{c1::ipsilateral leptomeningeal angiomatosis/cortical calcification}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: easy. These are classic findings in Sturge-Weber syndrome.

fields.Text: The annual occupational extremity dose limit for adult radiation workers is {{c1::50 rem (0.5 Sv)}}.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Adult radiation workers have an annual extremity dose limit of 50 rem (0.5 Sv) for areas distal to knees or elbows, which is ten times higher than the whole-body limit due to lower radiosensitivity and localized exposure risk.

fields.Text: Hirschsprung disease is congenital {{c1::aganglionosis of the rectum}} and may show a rectosigmoid ratio less than 1.
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Hirschsprung disease is caused by congenital aganglionosis of the rectum. Continued failure to pass meconium in a newborn warrants an abdominal X-ray, and a contrast enema should be performed if the diagnosis is suspected.

fields.Text:
Posterior calcaneal tuberosity avulsion at the Achilles insertion. Most common patient group?

{{c1::Older adults}}
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Achilles tendon avulsion fractures occur at posterior calcaneal tuberosity, commonly seen in older adults (insufficiency) and diabetic patients (neuropathic), caused by sudden plantarflexion with calf contraction.

fields.Text: Achilles tendon avulsion fractures occur at the posterior calcaneal tuberosity and are more common in {{c1::older adults and diabetics}}.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Achilles tendon avulsion fractures occur at posterior calcaneal tuberosity, commonly seen in older adults (insufficiency) and diabetic patients (neuropathic), caused by sudden plantarflexion with calf contraction.

fields.Text:
Cyanotic newborn with bowel in the chest and pulmonary hypoplasia. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Congenital diaphragmatic hernia}}
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH) is caused by a developmental defect in the diaphragm through which abdominal contents enter the chest. This results in pulmonary hypoplasia on the same side, which is often severe. Due to the mass effect, the mediastinum is usually shifted toward the opposite side, and that lung may be underdeveloped as well. CDH occurs more commonly on the left side. Immediate intubation at delivery is mandatory to avoid intrathoracic bowel inflation. Severe respiratory distress and pulmonary hypertension may require nitric oxide or ECMO. Surgical correction of the defect may be edge-to-edge or involve the placement of a patch.

fields.Text: Congenital diaphragmatic hernia causes ipsilateral {{c1::pulmonary hypoplasia}} from abdominal contents entering the chest.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Congenital diaphragmatic hernia (CDH) is caused by a developmental defect in the diaphragm through which abdominal contents enter the chest. This results in pulmonary hypoplasia on the same side, which is often severe. Due to the mass effect, the mediastinum is usually shifted toward the opposite side, and that lung may be underdeveloped as well. CDH occurs more commonly on the left side. Immediate intubation at delivery is mandatory to avoid intrathoracic bowel inflation. Severe respiratory distress and pulmonary hypertension may require nitric oxide or ECMO. Surgical correction of the defect may be edge-to-edge or involve the placement of a patch.

fields.Text:
Temporal bone image with arrow pointing to sharp spur at superior EAC/lateral tympanic cavity. Structure?

{{c1::Scutum}}
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Knowledge of the complex anatomy of the inner ear is useful in arriving at a diagnosis or appropriate differential diagnosis, as many processes are anatomically confined.

fields.Text: The scutum is the sharp bony spur commonly eroded early by an enlarging {{c1::cholesteatoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q42. Correct; difficulty: easy. Knowledge of the complex anatomy of the inner ear is useful in arriving at a diagnosis or appropriate differential diagnosis, as many processes are anatomically confined.

fields.Text:
Dorsal base avulsion fracture of distal phalanx after forced DIP flexion. Most likely injury?

{{c1::Mallet finger}}
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Mallet finger results from forced flexion of an extended finger and can present with bony avulsion or tendinous injury.

fields.Text: Mallet finger results from {{c1::forced flexion of an extended DIP joint}} and can cause dorsal distal phalanx avulsion.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Mallet finger results from forced flexion of an extended finger and can present with bony avulsion or tendinous injury.

fields.Text:
MR urogram with duplicated collecting system and incontinence. Most likely etiology?

{{c1::Ectopic insertion of the upper pole moiety ureter}}
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Maximum intensity projection images from an MR urogram demonstrate left ureteral duplication (blue arrows).

fields.Text: By the Weigert-Meyer rule, the upper pole moiety ureter inserts {{c1::ectopically inferomedial}} and is prone to obstruction.
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Maximum intensity projection images from an MR urogram demonstrate left ureteral duplication (blue arrows).

fields.Text: A forcing function is a safety design that {{c1::prevents a dangerous action from being performed}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: hard. Human factor engineering is a discipline the explores the challenges involving health care providers and safely using available medical equipment and technology.

fields.Text:
Cirrhotic patient with intractable ascites, IVC narrowing, and venous collaterals. Diagnosis and treatment?

{{c1::IVC stenosis; stent placement}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. Based on the provided images, there is marked narrowing of the inferior vena cava (blue arrow) with opacification of multiple collateral venous structures proximal to the stenosis (yellow arrows).

fields.Text: Hemodynamically significant IVC stenosis can cause refractory ascites and may be treated with {{c1::stent placement}}.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: easy. Based on the provided images, there is marked narrowing of the inferior vena cava (blue arrow) with opacification of multiple collateral venous structures proximal to the stenosis (yellow arrows).

fields.Text:
Sports injury with cervical ICA wall hematoma and severe luminal narrowing. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Carotid artery dissection}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The fat-saturated T1 image show a high signal intensity focus in the wall of the right cervical internal carotid artery (red arrow) with marked resultant narrowing of the vessel lumen (blue arrow).

fields.Text: Initial management of cervical carotid artery dissection is {{c1::antiplatelet therapy with or without anticoagulation plus ICU monitoring}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The fat-saturated T1 image show a high signal intensity focus in the wall of the right cervical internal carotid artery (red arrow) with marked resultant narrowing of the vessel lumen (blue arrow).

fields.Text: Cervical carotid dissection is initially managed medically with {{c1::antiplatelet therapy, with or without anticoagulation, and close neurologic monitoring}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. The fat-saturated T1 image show a high signal intensity focus in the wall of the right cervical internal carotid artery (red arrow) with marked resultant narrowing of the vessel lumen (blue arrow).

fields.Text:
ASD flow quantification by phase-contrast MRI. What imaging plane is needed?

{{c1::Plane perpendicular to flow across the ASD}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Phase-contrast imaging obtained perpendicular to the defect in end-systole provides optimal ASD flow quantification by maximizing flow measurement while minimizing septal motion artifacts.

fields.Text: Phase-contrast MRI should be prescribed {{c1::perpendicular to the expected flow direction}} for ASD flow quantification.
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Phase-contrast imaging obtained perpendicular to the defect in end-systole provides optimal ASD flow quantification by maximizing flow measurement while minimizing septal motion artifacts.

fields.Text: CT metal artifact can be decreased by reconstructing acquired images at {{c1::thicker slices}}, at the cost of spatial resolution.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Thick slices reduce metal artifacts through partial volume averaging but decrease spatial resolution - representing a trade-off between artifact reduction and image detail that's generally less preferred than advanced MAR algorithms or dual-energy techniques.

fields.Text: Reconstructing CT images with {{c1::thicker slices}} reduces metal artifact by partial-volume averaging but sacrifices detail.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Thick slices reduce metal artifacts through partial volume averaging but decrease spatial resolution - representing a trade-off between artifact reduction and image detail that's generally less preferred than advanced MAR algorithms or dual-energy techniques.

fields.Text:
Persistent left SVC shown on CT. Most common drainage site?

{{c1::Coronary sinus}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q50. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Persistent left superior vena cava most commonly drains into the right atrium via coronary sinus (asymptomatic) or less commonly into the left atrium causing right-to-left shunt.

fields.Text: Persistent left SVC most commonly drains to the right atrium through the {{c1::coronary sinus}}.
